bead.title: auth-error-mapping: AuthError → HTTP + observability envelope
bead.description:
Wire the full observability envelope for auth rejections: every AuthError gets a structured log event with a canonical field set, plus a metrics counter increment. B1 already landed AuthError::status_code() and AuthError::error_code() in axon-core, and AuthErrorResponse + IntoResponse in crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs. This bead extends that path — it does NOT duplicate it.

## Scope — exactly what to add

### 1. Add the metrics crate to the workspace

Add to root Cargo.toml under [workspace.dependencies]:
  metrics = "0.24"

Add to crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml under [dependencies]:
  metrics.workspace = true

The metrics crate provides a facade — the recording backend is configured at startup in main/serve. For this bead, just emit via metrics::counter!(); no global recorder wiring is required (the counter becomes a no-op if no recorder is installed, which is fine for tests).

### 2. Extend crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs

Add a helper at the end of the module:

    /// Observability envelope emitted on every auth rejection.
    ///
    /// Fields:
    /// - error_code: stable SDK-facing code from AuthError::error_code()
    /// - tenant_path: the URL's {tenant} segment when known, else "(unknown)"
    /// - database_path: the URL's {database} segment when known, else "(unknown)"
    /// - op: required op for the HTTP method ("read"|"write"|"admin"), when known
    /// - user_id_if_known: user UUID if sub resolved, else unset
    /// - jti_if_known: credential jti if JWT parsed, else unset
    /// - remote_addr: client IP as a string, when available
    fn record_auth_rejection(
        err: &AuthError,
        ctx: &AuthRejectionContext,
    ) { ... }

    /// Context passed from the verification pipeline to the observability
    /// helper. All fields are optional because a rejection can happen before
    /// any of them are known.
    #[derive(Default)]
    struct AuthRejectionContext {
        tenant_path: Option<String>,
        database_path: Option<String>,
        op: Option<Op>,
        user_id_if_known: Option<UserId>,
        jti_if_known: Option<Uuid>,
        remote_addr: Option<String>,
    }

record_auth_rejection does three things:
1. Emit a tracing::warn!() event with target="axon.auth.reject" and all the fields as structured kv pairs. Use the error_code as the event name / message, e.g. tracing::warn!(target: "axon.auth.reject", error_code = %err.error_code(), ...).
2. Increment metrics::counter!("axon_auth_rejections_total", "error_code" => err.error_code()).
3. Return nothing; side-effect only.

Update verify_request to build an AuthRejectionContext incrementally (populate fields as they become available: tenant_path once the path is extracted, jti once the JWT is parsed, user_id once sub is resolved) and call record_auth_rejection on every early-return.

### 3. Add scenario test: crates/axon-server/tests/auth_rejection_matrix.rs

Table-driven scenario test that walks every row of ADR-018 §4 failure table (14 rows). For each row:
1. Set up the minimal server state (in-memory storage, a test JwtIssuer with a fixed key)
2. Build the minimal defective request per the row
3. Invoke the middleware via axum's testing infra (you can reuse the pattern from auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs)
4. Assert: response.status matches ADR-018 §4 table, response body parses to { code: <expected>, ... }

Unlike B1's unit tests which test the middleware in isolation, this scenario test hits the full middleware-plus-handler stack and exercises the observability envelope (the counter and log event must both fire). You do not need a metrics recorder installed — counter!() with no recorder is a no-op, but the call itself must happen (verify via a simple atomic counter wrapper around metrics::counter! if testing the fire-rate is required; otherwise rely on tracing subscriber capture).

For the tracing side, install a tracing_subscriber::registry() with a capture layer (the tracing-subscriber test helper crate or a simple Vec<Event> capture) and assert that every rejection produces exactly one axon.auth.reject event with the expected fields.

### ADR-018 §4 failure table (NORMATIVE — copy into test cases)

| Failure | Status | error_code |
|---|---|---|
| No Authorization header | 401 | unauthenticated |
| Header not Bearer | 401 | credential_malformed |
| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | credential_malformed |
| aud as JSON array | 401 | credential_malformed |
| Signature invalid | 401 | credential_invalid |
| exp in past (beyond skew) | 401 | credential_expired |
| nbf in future (beyond skew) | 401 | credential_not_yet_valid |
| jti revoked | 401 | credential_revoked |
| iss unknown | 401 | credential_foreign_issuer |
| aud != URL tenant | 403 | credential_wrong_tenant |
| sub suspended/deleted | 401 | user_suspended |
| sub not a tenant member | 403 | not_a_tenant_member |
| URL database not in grants | 403 | database_not_granted |
| Op not in grant | 403 | op_not_granted |

## Out of scope (file follow-up beads, do NOT include here)

- INV-019 determinism workload in axon-sim. That's its own bead — axon-sim integration is bigger than the observability envelope wiring.
- Multi-backend (SQLite + Postgres + memory) determinism. For this bead, memory-backed tests are sufficient; backend conformance tests land in a separate pass.
- Metrics recorder wiring in serve.rs. That's a deployment-time concern and should be its own bead when the ops story needs it.

## Files to touch

- Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add metrics to [workspace.dependencies]
- crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml — add metrics.workspace = true
- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — add record_auth_rejection + AuthRejectionContext; wire into verify_request
- crates/axon-server/tests/auth_rejection_matrix.rs — new scenario test file
- Possibly crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies] — tracing-subscriber with 'env-filter' or 'test-util' features if the project doesn't already have a subscriber for tests

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs — AuthError is done; status_code/error_code are done
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — that's the Tailscale path, leave it
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes for this bead

## Verification

Run before committing:
- cargo test -p axon-server --test auth_rejection_matrix (must pass for all 14 rows)
- cargo test -p axon-server --test auth_pipeline_test (must still pass — this bead is additive to B1)
- cargo test -p axon-server --test auth_pipeline_integration_test (must still pass)
- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
bead.acceptance:
- [ ] metrics crate added to workspace and wired into crates/axon-server
- [ ] record_auth_rejection helper + AuthRejectionContext struct exist in auth_pipeline.rs
- [ ] verify_request calls record_auth_rejection on every early-return rejection path
- [ ] crates/axon-server/tests/auth_rejection_matrix.rs exists and has 14 table-driven test cases, one per ADR-018 §4 failure row
- [ ] Each test case asserts (HTTP status, error_code in response body, tracing event with expected fields)
- [ ] axon_auth_rejections_total counter is incremented on every rejection (testable via a capture recorder or the tracing subscriber)
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green (all 3 test files)
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] B1's existing auth_pipeline_test.rs and auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs still pass unchanged
